title: elymus trachycaulus subsp. major
intro: Elymus trachycaulus subsp. major is a subspecies of the Elymus trachycaulus grass, commonly known as slough grass or bearded wheatgrass, native to North America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues and soothing digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Elymus trachycaulus subsp. major include its ability to reduce inflammation, alleviate symptoms of bronchitis and asthma, and support the immune system. The herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to make teas, salves, and oils for topical application.
